mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding labor income that supports aggregate consumption and corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not positively correlated with month-over-month PAYEMS changes (Rank IC < 0.02) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the effective federal funds rate exceeds 2.0 percent, restrictive short-term borrowing costs reduce corporate investment and equity risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of months when FEDFUNDS > 2.0% over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and subdued consumer price inflation indicates robust real final demand without provoking monetary contraction, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 504440 and CPIAUCSL at 253.113 in early 2019
failure_condition: The joint z-score of RSAFS growth and CPIAUCSL stability fails to predict positive 60-day returns (Rank IC < 0.03) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion where unemployment is already below 4.5 percent, a month-over-month decline in the unemployment rate signals an overheating labor market that threatens corporate profit margins through accelerating wage costs, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not negative following month-over-month declines in UNRATE when UNRATE < 4.5% (hit rate < 50% over 3-year window).

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in industrial production signals accelerating manufacturing activity and capacity utilization that lifts cyclical earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are not positive in more than 55% of instances when INDPRO growth exceeds zero over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in housing starts signal rising residential investment and downstream demand for construction materials and consumer durables, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns show no positive correlation with month-over-month HOUST changes (Rank IC < 0.02) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a rising federal funds rate and a declining unemployment rate indicates monetary tightening into an already tight labor market, which rapidly increases recession risk and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.8% and FEDFUNDS at 2.4% in early 2019
failure_condition: The interaction term of FEDFUNDS changes and UNRATE changes does not produce negative 5-day returns (Rank IC > -0.02) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Year-over-year consumer price inflation exceeding 2.5 percent erodes real corporate pricing power and raises the probability of Federal Reserve tightening, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 50% of triggered months when CPIAUCSL year-over-year exceeds 2.5% over a 3-year rolling window.
